info.title: EduCollab API
info.description:
EduCollab workspace collaboration API (v1).

## Base URL and tenancy
- All routes are under `/api`.
- Authenticated workspace operations use the **current workspace** scope: `/api/workspace/...`.
- Users may belong to multiple workspaces; `Users.WorkspaceId` stores the active workspace used by current-workspace routes.
- List memberships with `GET /api/users/me/workspaces`; switch active workspace with `PUT /api/users/me/active-workspace`.
- Platform administration uses `/api/admin/...`.

## Authentication
- Send `Authorization: Bearer {accessToken}` on protected routes.
- Obtain tokens from `POST /api/users/login`, `POST /api/users/login/confirm-code`, or `POST /api/users/registration-confirm`.

## List query parameters
Collection list endpoints accept optional query parameters:

| Parameter | Default | Rules |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| `page` | 1 | Must be >= 1. Invalid values return `400` with `error: invalid_pagination`. |
| `pageSize` | 20 | Must be 1–100. Invalid values return `400` with `error: invalid_pagination`. |
| `sort` | per resource (see below) | Ascending: `sort=name`. Descending: `sort=-createdAt`. Unknown fields return `400` with `error: invalid_sort`. |

### Allowed `sort` fields by resource
- **Assets, scenes, groups, flows, admin workspaces**: `name`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `id` (default `name` ascending).
- **Workspace / group members**: `userId`, `joinedAt`, `role` where applicable (default `joinedAt` ascending).
- **Workspace creation requests**: `name`, `createdAt`, `status`, `id` (default `createdAt` descending).

Paged list responses include `page`, `pageSize`, and `totalCount` alongside the collection.

### Collection filters (normalized URLs)
- **Groups** `GET /api/workspace/groups`: tree browse with optional `parentId`. Root browse (`parentId` omitted) returns entry groups the caller may open (direct membership plus subgroups whose parent is not accessible). Drill down with `parentId={groupId}`. `GET /api/workspace/groups/flat` returns all accessible groups in one list (group pickers, search, admin tables). Adding a user to a group also adds them to all subgroups.
- **Group library** `GET /api/workspace/groups/{groupId}/assets|scenes|flows`: resources placed in that group (requires effective group access).
- **Assets** `GET /api/workspace/assets`: union of all accessible assets. Optional filter: `owner=me`. Each asset has group placement via `groupId` / `groupIds`; change placement with `PUT /api/workspace/assets/{assetId}`. Binary content is a ZIP file via `PUT /api/workspace/assets/{assetId}/content`.
- **Scenes** `GET /api/workspace/scenes`: union of all accessible scenes. Optional filter: `owner=me`. CRUD by id at `/api/workspace/scenes/{sceneId}`. Create/update send inline `jsonContent` as JSON or upload a `.json` file via `multipart/form-data` (`jsonFile` part).
- **Flows** `GET /api/workspace/flows`: union of all accessible flows. Optional filter: `owner=me`. CRUD by id at `/api/workspace/flows/{flowId}`.
- **Scene assets** `GET /api/workspace/scene-assets`: required `sceneId` (authoritative manifest of assets required to render the scene). Attach via `POST /api/workspace/scene-assets`; detach via `DELETE /api/workspace/scene-assets?sceneId=&assetId=`. Download ZIP content in scene context via `GET /api/workspace/scene-assets/content?sceneId=&assetId=` (required when `canViewDirectly` is false).
- **Flow scenes** `GET /api/workspace/flow-scenes`: required `flowId` (authoritative manifest of scenes in the flow). Attach via `POST /api/workspace/flow-scenes`; detach via `DELETE /api/workspace/flow-scenes?flowId=&sceneId=`. Load scene JSON in flow context via `GET /api/workspace/flow-scenes/content?flowId=&sceneId=` (required when `canViewDirectly` is false).

## Scene runtime asset loading
Clients rendering a scene should:
1. `GET /api/workspace/scenes/{sceneId}` — load `jsonContent` (object graph; objects use `assetId` to point at workspace assets).
2. `GET /api/workspace/scene-assets?sceneId=` — server-resolved manifest with `usableInScene` and `canViewDirectly` flags.
3. Download each required asset ZIP:
   - When `canViewDirectly` is true: `GET /api/workspace/assets/{assetId}/content`.
   - When `canViewDirectly` is false but `usableInScene` is true: `GET /api/workspace/scene-assets/content?sceneId=&assetId=` (scene-context only).
4. Cross-check every `assetId` parsed from `jsonContent` against the manifest; ids missing from the manifest are broken or inaccessible references.

Scene-context access allows runtime use of assets referenced by a visible scene without granting standalone asset library access.

## Flow runtime scene loading
Clients running a flow should:
1. `GET /api/workspace/flows/{flowId}` — load flow metadata.
2. `GET /api/workspace/flow-scenes?flowId=` — server-resolved manifest with `usableInFlow` and `canViewDirectly` flags.
3. Load each attached scene JSON:
   - When `canViewDirectly` is true: `GET /api/workspace/scenes/{sceneId}`.
   - When `canViewDirectly` is false but `usableInFlow` is true: `GET /api/workspace/flow-scenes/content?flowId=&sceneId=` (flow-context only).

Flow-context access allows runtime use of scenes attached to a visible flow without granting standalone scene library access.

## Errors
All `4xx` and `5xx` responses use RFC 9457 Problem Details (`application/problem+json`).
Common properties: `type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`, `error`, `requestId`.
Model validation failures use `error: validation_failed` and an `errors` object keyed by field name (camelCase).
Invalid scene asset references on create/update use `error: invalid_asset_reference` with an `invalidAssetReferences` array of `{ assetId, reason }`.
Every response includes an `X-Request-Id` header matching `requestId` in error bodies when present.

## Contract source
This document is generated from the running API (Swashbuckle). The committed copy lives at `openapi/v1/openapi.json`.
Regenerate with `scripts/export-openapi.ps1`.

get.summary: List workspace creation requests for platform admin review.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status where supported. Invalid values return `invalid_status`.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field. Prefix with `-` for descending (e.g. `-createdAt`). Allowed fields vary by resource; see API description. Invalid fields return `invalid_sort`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page index. Default: 1. Invalid values return `invalid_pagination`.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Page size. Default: 20, maximum: 100. Invalid values return `invalid_pagination`.

403.description: Caller is not a platform administrator.

post.summary: Approve a pending workspace creation request and email the requester an approval token.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Creation request identifier.

403.description: Caller is not a platform administrator.

post.summary: Deny a pending workspace creation request and email the requester.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Creation request identifier.

403.description: Caller is not a platform administrator.

get.summary: List scenes attached to a flow.
get.description:
Authoritative manifest for running a flow. Each entry includes `usableInFlow` and
`canViewDirectly` flags for scene access in flow context. Use M:EduCollab.Api.Controllers.FlowScenesController.GetFlowSceneContent(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
to load scene JSON when `canViewDirectly` is false.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: Flow identifier (required).

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field. Prefix with `-` for descending (e.g. `-createdAt`). Allowed fields vary by resource; see API description. Invalid fields return `invalid_sort`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page index. Default: 1. Invalid values return `invalid_pagination`.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Page size. Default: 20, maximum: 100. Invalid values return `invalid_pagination`.

parameters.name: sceneId
parameters.description: Scene identifier (required).

get.summary: Load scene JSON in flow context.

Use when the manifest lists a scene with `usableInFlow: true` and `canViewDirectly: false`.
Standalone `GET /scenes/{sceneId}` remains direct-access only.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: Flow identifier (required).

parameters.name: sceneId
parameters.description: Scene identifier (required).

404.description: Flow, scene, or content was not found.

get.summary: List assets attached to a scene.
get.description:
Authoritative manifest for rendering a scene. Merges assets referenced by `assetId` in scene JSON
with explicit scene attachments. Use M:EduCollab.Api.Controllers.SceneAssetsController.GetSceneAssetContent(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken) to download ZIP content when
`canViewDirectly` is false.

parameters.name: sceneId
parameters.description: Scene identifier (required).

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field. Prefix with `-` for descending (e.g. `-createdAt`). Allowed fields vary by resource; see API description. Invalid fields return `invalid_sort`.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page index. Default: 1. Invalid values return `invalid_pagination`.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Page size. Default: 20, maximum: 100. Invalid values return `invalid_pagination`.

400.description: Invalid scene id, sort, or pagination.

post.summary: Attach an asset to a scene.

201.description: Asset was attached to the scene.

404.description: Scene or asset was not found.

delete.summary: Detach an asset from a scene.

parameters.name: sceneId
parameters.description: Scene identifier (required).

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: Asset identifier (required).

204.description: Asset was detached from the scene.

get.summary: Download asset ZIP content in scene context.
get.description:
Use when the manifest lists an asset with `usableInScene: true` and `canViewDirectly: false`.
Standalone `GET /assets/{assetId}/content` remains direct-access only.

parameters.name: sceneId
parameters.description: Scene identifier (required).

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: Asset identifier (required).

404.description: Scene, asset, or content was not found.

get.summary: List groups a scene is shared with.

post.summary: Share a scene with a group.

put.summary: Replace all group shares for a scene.

delete.summary: Remove a scene from a group.

post.summary: Create a new scene in the specified group.
post.description:
Send `jsonContent` inline in the JSON body. Scene objects reference workspace assets via an `assetId`
property anywhere in the JSON tree. Use M:EduCollab.Api.Controllers.ScenesController.CreateSceneFromForm(EduCollab.Api.Requests.Scenes.CreateSceneFormRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken) to upload a `.json` file instead.

requestBody.description: Scene creation payload including target `groupId` and inline `jsonContent`.

400.description: Scene could not be created or references an invalid asset.

403.description: Caller cannot create scenes in this group.

post.summary: Register a new user. A confirmation email is sent; sign-in is blocked until email is confirmed.

201.description: Registration succeeded and a confirmation email was sent.

post.summary: Confirm email after registration; returns access and refresh tokens for immediate sign-in.

200.description: Email was confirmed and tokens were issued.

400.description: Confirmation token was invalid or expired.

post.summary: Resend the confirmation email for an unconfirmed account. For privacy, the same response is returned even when the email does not exist.
Send a one-time 6-digit sign-in code to the confirmed email address.

200.description: The sign-in code was sent to the confirmed email address.

401.description: No account exists for the provided email address.

post.summary: Log in a user with a previously sent 6-digit sign-in code.

200.description: The sign-in code was valid and tokens were issued.

401.description: The sign-in code was invalid, expired, or locked after too many attempts.

post.summary: Log in a user with provided credentials.

200.description: User logged in. Returns a new access token.

401.description: Invalid login attempt. Returns an error message.

post.summary: Login with a refresh token to obtain a new access token.

401.description: Invalid refresh token or user is unauthorized.

get.summary: Retrieve the current authenticated user's information.

403.description: User is forbidden from accessing this resource.

get.summary: List workspaces the current user belongs to, including the active workspace id.

200.description: Workspace memberships for the current user.

put.summary: Set the caller's active workspace for current-workspace routes.

requestBody.description: Target workspace id. Caller must be a member of the workspace.

200.description: Active workspace was updated; returns the current user profile.

400.description: Invalid workspace id or update failed.

403.description: Caller is not a member of the workspace.

get.summary: Retrieve opaque web-client preferences for the current user.
get.description:
The backend treats this document as opaque JSON and stores it outside the database.
It is intended for web-client profile preferences only.

put.summary: Replace opaque web-client preferences for the current user.
put.description: The request body is stored as opaque JSON without schema binding or validation.

get.summary: Retrieve the user's profile by id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: User Id.

403.description: Caller cannot access this user id.

put.summary: Update user information by user Id. The id must match the authenticated user (JWT subject).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: User Id.

403.description: Caller cannot update this user id.

delete.summary: Delete user by user Id. The id must match the authenticated user (JWT subject).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: User Id.

post.summary: Reset password confirm.
Reset password.
Change password.

instance.description: Request path that produced the error.

requestId.description: Server-generated request identifier; also returned as X-Request-Id.

ApiProblemDetails.description: RFC 9457 problem response emitted by EduCollab API endpoints.

instance.description: Request path that produced the error.

requestId.description: Server-generated request identifier; also returned as X-Request-Id.

errors.description: Field-level validation messages keyed by camelCase field name.

Bearer.description: JWT bearer token. In Swagger UI, use Authorize and paste the access token from login.
